questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Notion Academy as described in the article?

options.text: To provide badges and certifications for learning Notion
options.explanation: The article states that Notion Academy helps you earn badges and become a certified admin, making this the main purpose.

options.text: To sell Notion templates in the marketplace
options.explanation: Selling templates is mentioned as a lesson topic, not the overarching purpose of the Academy.

options.text: To offer technical support for Notion bugs
options.explanation: Technical support is handled by the Help Center, not by Notion Academy.

options.text: To host community forums for Notion users
options.explanation: Community involvement is encouraged, but the Academy itself is focused on structured learning, not forums.

questions.question: Which plan type is required to become a Notion Certified Admin?

options.explanation: The article explicitly says certification is available for any plan type.

options.text: Any plan type, regardless of the company size
options.explanation: The certification is open to users on any Notion plan, making this the correct answer.
Business plan is not a requirement; the certification is broader.
Free users can also become certified, but the answer that covers all plans is more accurate.

questions.question: Which lesson is categorized as Intermediate and focuses on building an intake system with forms, charts, and automations?

options.explanation: This lesson is listed as Beginner, not Intermediate.

options.text: An issue tracker for any team
options.explanation: The description matches the Intermediate lesson about building an intake system.
That lesson focuses on teamwork, not intake systems, and is marked Beginner.
Workspace design is an Advanced lesson, unrelated to intake systems.

questions.question: How many lessons are included in the "Essentials Path"?

options.explanation: The path contains more than a single lesson.
The article specifies that the Essentials Path consists of 3 lessons.
Five lessons correspond to the Advanced Path, not Essentials.
There is no mention of a 7‑lesson path.

questions.question: What functionality do Custom Agents provide within Notion?

options.text: Create new Notion templates for the marketplace
options.explanation: Custom Agents automate work; they do not create marketplace templates.

options.text: Automate repeat work and reduce everyday busywork for your team
options.explanation: The description of Custom Agents highlights automation and busywork reduction.

options.text: Design the visual layout of a Notion workspace
options.explanation: Workspace design is a separate lesson, not a function of Custom Agents.
While useful, deadline tracking is not the primary purpose of Custom Agents.

questions.question: Which path is designed for advanced users and includes five lessons?

options.explanation: Essentials Path is for beginners and has three lessons.
Workflows Path is intermediate and contains four lessons.
The article lists the Advanced Path as having five lessons for advanced users.
There is no separate "Beginner Path" listed; beginner content is under Essentials.

questions.question: If a user wants to learn best practices for designing a Notion workspace, which specific lesson should they take?

options.explanation: Project management focuses on tasks and projects, not workspace design.
The lesson titled "Workspace design" directly addresses best‑practice design for workspaces.
That lesson is about creating and selling templates, not workspace layout.
This lesson focuses on building connected systems, not design principles.

questions.question: Which path description explicitly mentions the use of AI to create connected systems?

options.explanation: Essentials Path focuses on basic skills, with no AI reference.
The Workflows Path description says "Create connected systems that unite teams and projects, with the help of AI."
Advanced Path covers automation and architecture but does not specifically call out AI in its description.
Custom Agents automate work but the AI reference is made at the path level, not in the lesson title.
